commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0e4f9676bd3309c46c7f38fa00ef7c06a0a51830)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds per-agent QMD mcporter config and daemon routing, extensive memory regression coverage and documentation, an empty memory-core doctor compatibility scaffold, and Skill Workshop localization changes.

PR surface: Source +771, Tests +1645, Docs +24. Total +2440 across 43 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main shares mcporter daemon-start state across managers, and the proposed probe defects reproduce deterministically when home/project define the same server or MCPORTER_CONFIG omits it while another layer contains it. A full live two-agent collision was not independently run.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The blockers are concrete and mechanical: repair two config-resolution rules, remove the duplicated UI commit, and add focused tests before contributor-supplied real behavior proof is refreshed.

target.title: Memory/QMD: isolate mcporter sidecars per agent
target.body:
Supersedes #79745 — the original PR was accidentally closed when its source branch was deleted. This is the same change set, rebased onto the latest `main` and extended with the fixes from the ClawSweeper review.

## Summary

- Isolate QMD mcporter daemon/config state per agent by writing an agent-scoped mcporter config and passing it to mcporter calls.
- Preserve the configured mcporter server definition instead of replacing custom/remote server config with a hard-coded local `qmd mcp` entry.
- Keep QMD model/env settings flowing only through the generated stdio server entry, while stripping QMD-scoped XDG env from mcporter's own process environment.
- Tolerate mcporter daemon log lines before JSON responses so warm daemon calls do not break memory search parsing.
- **Added:** Detect `logging.daemon.enabled` on mcporter stdio servers to preserve daemon logging config.
- **Added:** Read project-scoped `config/mcporter.json` from workspace directory as an additional mcporter config layer.
- **Added:** Doctor contract scaffold for memory-core plugin.
- **Added:** Public docs explaining mcporter integration and config discovery order.

## reviewMetrics

```
configSurfaces:
  added: 0
  changed: 1    # mcporter probe routing: now reads project config + detects daemon logging
  removed: 0
userVisibleBehavior:
  changed: 2    # (1) logging.daemon.enabled no longer silently dropped
                # (2) project-scoped config/mcporter.json is now consulted
whyItMatters:
  - Existing mcporter setups with project config/mcporter.json are preserved
  - Users who customized daemon logging keep their settings across upgrades
  - Config discovery order now matches mcporter's documented behavior
```

## Changes since #79745

- Rebased onto latest `openclaw/main` (was 399 commits behind).
- Resolved `doctor-contract-api.ts` merge conflict: upstream now ships dreaming-state migrations in the same file, so the memory-core doctor contract appends `legacyConfigRules` / `normalizeCompatibilityConfig` alongside them.
- Extended raw mcporter config probe to detect `logging.daemon.enabled` (not just top-level `logging.enabled`).
- Extended raw mcporter config candidate list to include `<workspaceDir>/config/mcporter.json`.
- Added focused regression tests for the two new detection paths and test-suite hermeticity via `HOME=tmpRoot`.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.ts docs/concepts/memory-qmd.md`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm lint:extensions`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** QMD memory search through mcporter must keep each agent on its own mcporter config/daemon state while preserving a configured stdio `qmd` server even when mcporter stores the command as an absolute executable path.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux with Node `v24.15.0`, `mcporter 0.10.2`, `qmd 2.1.0`, and the configured mcporter `qmd` stdio server whose command is an absolute qmd binary path. Home/state/workspace paths are redacted.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
node --version
mcporter --version
qmd --version
mcporter config get qmd --json
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm tsgo:extensions
pnpm lint:extensions
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
node --version -> v24.15.0
mcporter --version -> 0.10.2
qmd --version -> qmd 2.1.0 (bab86d5)

mcporter config get qmd --json
{
  "name": "qmd",
  "source": { "kind": "local", "path": "~/.mcporter/mcporter.json" },
  "transport": "stdio",
  "command": "~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd",
  "args": ["mcp"]
}

Focused tests:
- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts: 138 tests passed

Type/lint checks:
- pnpm tsgo:extensions completed successfully
- pnpm lint:extensions completed successfully
```

**Observed result after fix:** The manager classified the absolute-command mcporter `qmd` server as `generated`, wrote separate per-agent mcporter configs, preserved the absolute qmd command and `["mcp"]` args, and injected per-agent `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, `QMD_CONFIG_DIR`, and `XDG_CACHE_HOME` values.

**What was not tested:** Live remote HTTP mcporter server; preservation path is covered by focused unit tests. Full `openclaw memory ...` CLI was not exercised because the local test checkout intentionally runs with a stub config.
